overwhelmed.title: You're feeling overwhelmed
overwhelmed.validation: That makes sense. When too much is happening at once, your nervous system can start to feel overloaded.
overwhelmed.tip: Pause and take one slow breath in, then a longer breath out. Let your shoulders drop.
overwhelmed.reminder: You do not have to solve everything in this moment.
overwhelmed.nextStep: Choose one small next step: get water, sit down, or give yourself 30 seconds before responding.

angry.title: You're feeling angry
angry.validation: Anger often shows up when something feels unfair, stressful, or too much to carry.
angry.tip: Unclench your jaw and your hands. Step back if you can before responding.
angry.reminder: Your child may need your calm even when their behavior is hard.
angry.nextStep: Use a short pause phrase like: 'I need a moment, then I’ll help.'

sad.title: You're feeling sad
sad.validation: Sadness can make everything feel heavier, especially when you're still trying to care for someone else.
sad.tip: Put a hand on your chest and take one steady breath. Let yourself soften for a moment.
sad.reminder: You are still a caring parent, even on hard days.
sad.nextStep: Choose one gentle action: sit with your child, lower expectations, or ask for support.

okay.title: You're feeling okay
okay.validation: Okay is enough. You do not have to be at your best to stay connected.
okay.tip: Check in with your body and notice what feels steady right now.
okay.nextStep: Use this moment to connect in one simple way: eye contact, a kind word, or a calm touch.

hopeful.title: You're feeling hopeful
hopeful.validation: Hope can be a powerful starting place, even if things are still hard.
hopeful.tip: Take a breath and notice what feels possible right now.
hopeful.reminder: Connection grows through small, repeated moments.
hopeful.nextStep: Pick one positive action you want to practice with your child today.
